message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker — assign bd-mjh3.2.2 to PearlTower

Concurrent agent assigned PearlTower to bead bd-mjh3.2.2 (Formal Hook and
Effect Semantics Contract) with updated timestamp reflecting work pickup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -766,7 +766,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.19.4","title":"[FRX-19.4] Automated Rollback/Safe-Mode Synthesizer from Twin Counterfactual Evidence","description":"Compile counterfactual and stability outputs into deterministic rollback and safe-mode policy bundles that can be applied quickly during incidents or failed promotions.

Execution scope:
- define synthesis rules from counterfactual/boundary evidence to concrete policy deltas,
- prove non-regression constraints against constitutional compatibility invariants,
- generate signed rollback/safe-mode bundles with replay verification hooks,
- integrate synthesized bundles into milestone gate and incident command workflows.

Alien-artifact artifacts:
- rollback synthesis rulebook,
- policy-bundle compiler outputs,
- gate-ready replay verification reports.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Synthesis rules deterministically map twin evidence to rollback/safe-mode policy deltas.
2. Generated bundles satisfy constitutional compatibility and safety invariants.
3. Bundle artifacts are signed, replay-verifiable, and reversible.
4. Milestone and incident workflows can consume bundles automatically with fail-closed behavior on verification errors.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:44:47.766735070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:33:38.893586096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenreact-sidecar","policy-synthesis","replay","rollback","safe-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:30.905011666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.12.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:30.722437923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:30.541144834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.2","title":"[FRX-02] Semantic Compatibility Corpus and Contract Layer","description":"Build the semantic truth source for drop-in compatibility.

Focus:
- harvest real React behavior corpus,
- codify hook/effect semantics and edge cases,
- define strict compatibility contracts across React versions and browser/runtime surfaces,
- classify where compile-time optimization is legal vs where fallback must trigger.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Behavior corpus spans core hooks, concurrent features, suspense/hydration, context, refs, portals, error boundaries.
2. Semantic contracts are machine-checkable and tied to test fixtures.
3. Explicit fallback envelope exists for unsupported/ambiguous semantics.
4. Cross-version compatibility matrix is established.

Program-Wide Test Gate:
- Every child task must define comprehensive unit-test scope, end-to-end scenario scripts, and detailed structured logging artifacts.
- Feature closure requires linked evidence that unit/e2e suites passed with deterministic fixtures and replay/triage logs.
- Any missing or stale test/logging evidence blocks milestone promotion and release gating.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:41:57.259802239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:53.867384406Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","phase-core","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:29.173876085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:29.327090177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:29.483567286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:29.609033609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.2.1","title":"[FRX-02.1] Canonical React Behavior Corpus and Trace Fixtures","description":"Build the behavior corpus that represents “real React”.

Collect and normalize fixtures for:
- hooks (state/effect/memo/ref/reducer/context),
- concurrent rendering behaviors,
- suspense/error boundaries,
- hydration and server/client boundaries,
- portal/ref edge cases.

Each fixture includes expected observable traces.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Corpus includes high-risk semantics and edge cases.
2. Fixtures are deterministic and replayable.
3. Observable traces are versioned artifacts.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:43:59.699315447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:26:48.505618266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","react-compiler"]}
-{"id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","title":"[FRX-02.2] Formal Hook and Effect Semantics Contract","description":"Formalize hook/effect semantics as a machine-readable contract.

Define:
- hook slot indexing invariants,
- effect dependency semantics,
- scheduling/timing boundaries observable to user code,
- legal transformations preserving semantics.

Use typestate-style phase constraints to prevent illegal lowering states.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Hook/effect semantics are encoded in a contract document + tests.
2. Legal transformation rules are explicit.
3. Typestate or equivalent phase-safety constraints are present.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:00.069985342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:28.660830589Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:01.190125259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","title":"[FRX-02.2] Formal Hook and Effect Semantics Contract","description":"Formalize hook/effect semantics as a machine-readable contract.

Define:
- hook slot indexing invariants,
- effect dependency semantics,
- scheduling/timing boundaries observable to user code,
- legal transformations preserving semantics.

Use typestate-style phase constraints to prevent illegal lowering states.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Hook/effect semantics are encoded in a contract document + tests.
2. Legal transformation rules are explicit.
3. Typestate or equivalent phase-safety constraints are present.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:00.069985342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T20:18:57.758461408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:01.190125259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.2.3","title":"[FRX-02.3] Unsupported Semantics, Escape Hatches, and Deterministic Fallback Rules","description":"Define escape-hatch policy for unsupported/ambiguous semantics.

Deliver:
- precise fallback triggers,
- diagnostics to explain why compile path was rejected,
- compatibility-preserving execution route for unsupported patterns,
- guidance for incremental hardening of unsupported cases.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unsupported cases are explicit and justified.
2. Fallback behavior is deterministic and user-safe.
3. Diagnostic output is actionable.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:00.448890876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:28.523595747Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:01.365075728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.2.4","title":"[FRX-02.4] Cross-Version Compatibility Matrix (React/Browser/API Surfaces)","description":"Create the cross-version compatibility matrix.

Map behavior and support status for:
- React 18 vs React 19 semantics,
- browser API interactions that affect rendering/effects,
- edge API families (legacy class components, portals, refs, concurrent primitives).

Matrix must drive both test selection and release claims.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Version matrix exists with per-feature status.
2. Matrix rows are linked to fixtures/tests.
3. Release claims reference matrix evidence.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:00.832904926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:11.347073449Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-semantics","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:01.537609626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:44:01.712424744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.20","title":"[FRX-20] Comprehensive Test Matrix and Evidence-Grade Logging Enforcement Program","description":"Program intent: ensure every FRX workstream ships with comprehensive unit tests, deterministic end-to-end scripts, and evidence-grade logging artifacts that make failures reproducible and diagnosable.

Scope:
- define program-wide unit/e2e taxonomy and fixture standards,
- establish deterministic, high-signal logging schema for test and e2e runs,
- enforce CI gates for coverage, flake control, and artifact completeness,
- bind milestone/release promotion to test+logging evidence quality.

Why this matters:
Without a dedicated enforcement spine, testing/logging requirements can drift into soft guidance. FRX-20 makes them hard delivery obligations.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Program-wide test taxonomy and fixture standards are machine-readable and versioned.
2. Every FRX lane has explicit unit and e2e obligations with deterministic pass/fail criteria.
3. Test/e2e logging schema includes high-fidelity debugging fields and artifact retention rules.
4. Milestone and release gates fail closed on missing/stale test or logging evidence.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:26:19.420845419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:33:20.122757805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","execution-swarm","frankenreact-sidecar","logging","react-compiler","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:19.751375018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:19.865929931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:19.982947931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:20.114053762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:20.241110510Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.20","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.20.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:32:20.360080206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
